frame_analyses.description: Dr. Palak Paliwal introduces herself as a pediatric surgeon via talking head segment with social media branding
FLS peg transfer trainer displayed with 6 blue rubber balls, two needle drivers visible, red control boxes on sides
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Standard FLS trainer setup clearly shown for educational context

frame_analyses.description: Both needle drivers positioned to pick up a ball from the blue cup (left side of trainer)
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Bimanual grasper coordination demonstrated; drivers approach from convergent angle

frame_analyses.description: Ball transferred in mid-air between the two graspers; both instruments holding the object simultaneously
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth handoff technique shown; minimal instrument wobble

frame_analyses.description: Ball being placed onto a peg on the right side of the trainer
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Controlled placement with proper deceleration; no collision or dropped object

frame_analyses.description: Caption overlay states 'Instruments ka unnecessary crossing' indicating a common error being highlighted
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instructional emphasis on avoiding crossed instruments and inefficient paths

frame_analyses.description: Educational text overlay in Hindi/Hinglish listing training tips: smaller balls use, target size reduction, time-based practice, non-dominant hand task completion
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Explicit teaching content for skill development hierarchy and practice strategy

frame_analyses.description: Return to talking head closing remarks from Dr. Paliwal; soft background and professional framing

confidence_rationale: Video classified as instructional content with explicit educational narration, teaching overlays, and social media branding. Content is demonstrative in nature but not a formal performance evaluation. Per FLS rubric protocol, instructional videos are not scored for performance metrics.
cannot_determine: Actual completion time — demonstration segments are deliberately paced for instruction, not timed performance
Penalties — intentional demonstrations of both correct technique and error avoidance shown for learning purposes
Performance classification — this is educator content, not trainee performance assessment

demonstrated_techniques: Bimanual grasper coordination for object pickup
Mid-air transfer handoff between needle drivers
Controlled ball placement on peg with proper deceleration
Non-dominant hand initiation of task flow
verbal_cues: Social media engagement (YouTube, Facebook, Instagram promotion)
Professional credibility establishment (Dr. Palak Paliwal, Pediatric Surgeon)
common_errors_addressed: Unnecessary crossing of instruments (explicitly captioned in video)
practice_progression_recommendations: Start with smaller ball size to improve precision
Reduce target size to increase difficulty
Complete full task using non-dominant hand for laterality training

video_type_notes: Educational YouTube content designed for FLS learner instruction and technique coaching. Content uses demonstration, error-highlighting overlays, and summarized practice tips in both English and Hindi. Suitable for learners at novice-to-intermediate level seeking foundational peg transfer understanding.
